key_highlights: S&P 500 posts eighth consecutive weekly gain, longest since 2023, driven by AI stocks and IPO excitement.
New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh sworn in; hawkish stance and rising rate hike probability (Fed's Waller hints at hike) pressure bonds and gold.
U.S.-Iran peace deal uncertainty boosts oil prices, while peace hopes lift EM stocks and currencies.
SpaceX IPO filing reveals $2 trillion valuation ambition, Musk's control, and xAI losses; IPO market heats up.
Walmart warns of consumer spending cuts due to higher petrol prices, signaling K-shaped retail economy.
Copper surges on Trafigura's LME withdrawals and US premium pull; gold drops on rate hike fears.
Private credit defaults hit record high as interest rates soar; bond yields globally at near two-decade highs.
U.S. to award $2 billion to quantum-computing firms (IBM, others) with equity stakes.
Spotify and UMG announce AI covers/remixes licensing deal; Visa tests stablecoin settlement.
Morrisons plans to close 100 stores; Stellantis launches $70B plan with 60 new models.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum with AI stocks powering S&P 500 rally; Nvidia targets next wave of AI growth with new data center chips; SpaceX IPO filing highlights AI integration; U.S. government invests $2B in quantum computing firms (IBM, others).
sectors.implications: AI and quantum computing remain core growth drivers. IPO market (SpaceX) adds speculative froth. Investors should focus on companies with clear AI monetization paths and government contracts.

sectors.trend: Oil volatile on U.S.-Iran peace deal uncertainty; copper rises on Trafigura's LME withdrawals and US premium; gold drops on Fed rate hike fears (Waller, Warsh). US oil rig count up most in four years.
sectors.implications: Energy sector faces geopolitical risk and demand uncertainty. Copper benefits from supply squeeze and green transition. Gold under pressure from hawkish Fed; safe-haven demand may shift to bonds or cash.

sectors.trend: K-shaped economy: Walmart warns of spending cuts due to higher petrol prices; Morrisons closing 100 stores; Lay's/Doritos raising prices. Meanwhile, Airbnb expands beyond rentals; Stellantis launches $70B plan with 60 new models. Jet fuel hedgers vindicated.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary bifurcated: low-end pressured, high-end resilient. Retailers with pricing power (Walmart) may pass costs, but volume risk rises. Auto sector sees long-term EV/ICE transition investment.

sectors.trend: Private credit defaults hit record high as interest rates soar; muni bond sales surge; bond yields globally at near two-decade highs. Fed rate hike probability rising under Warsh.
sectors.implications: Rising rates pressure leveraged borrowers (private credit, high-yield). Muni bonds offer relative safety. Banks may benefit from higher net interest margins but face credit risk. Credit entry points attractive per Steinbach.

sectors.trend: Lantheus weighs $7B sale; Gilead/Kite present new oncology data at ASCO/EHA; Incyte's AI-powered R&D partnerships change investment story.
sectors.implications: M&A activity in biotech continues. AI-driven drug discovery gaining traction. Oncology remains a key focus. Investors should monitor clinical trial readouts and regulatory decisions.

sectors.trend: EM stocks and currencies head for weekly gain as peace hopes grow (U.S.-Iran). Dollar stalls on risk sentiment.
sectors.implications: Peace deal optimism supports EM risk assets. However, Fed hawkishness and strong dollar could reverse gains. Selective exposure to commodity exporters (copper, oil) may benefit.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin volatility hits 7-month low as institutional demand steadies; Ripple teams up on post-quantum upgrade; SEC delays prediction market ETFs and crypto versions of US stocks.
sectors.implications: Crypto markets maturing with institutional inflows. Regulatory uncertainty persists (SEC delays). Stablecoins gaining traction (Visa tests settlement). Quantum computing poses long-term risk to crypto.

sectors.trend: CAE says defense deals and cost cutting to drive profit surge; FBI prepares for drone threats at World Cup; SpaceX IPO filing highlights rockets-to-AI vision.
sectors.implications: Defense spending remains robust globally. Drone and counter-drone technologies are growth areas. SpaceX IPO could be a major catalyst for space/defense sector.

key_themes: Fed Hawkish Shift Under Warsh: Rate hike probability rising, bond yields surging, gold dropping.
Geopolitical Crosscurrents: U.S.-Iran peace deal uncertainty vs. peace hopes boosting EM and oil.
AI & IPO Frenzy: SpaceX IPO filing, Nvidia's next wave, quantum computing investment.
Consumer Squeeze: K-shaped retail, Walmart warning, Morrisons closures, rising fuel costs.
Commodity Supply Tightness: Copper withdrawals, oil rig count up, jet fuel hedgers win.
Private Credit Stress: Record defaults as interest rates soar.
Regulatory Landscape: SEC delays crypto ETFs, quantum computing equity stakes.
Global Bond Market Pressure: Long bond yields at near two-decade highs, UK and Japan under scrutiny.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate instruments; increase cash allocation; hedge with interest rate swaps or short Treasury futures.
Maintain diversified commodity exposure; hedge oil positions with options; increase exposure to defense stocks (CAE) and EM currencies on peace optimism.
Overweight discount retailers (Walmart) and essential goods; underweight discretionary luxury; monitor fuel prices and wage growth.
Reduce exposure to private credit funds and high-yield bonds; favor investment-grade credit; monitor BDC and CLO performance.
Short long-duration bonds; favor TIPS or floating-rate notes; increase cash and short-term Treasuries; hedge currency risk in UK/Japan.
Avoid chasing IPO hype without fundamental analysis; consider post-IPO lockup expiry risks; focus on companies with clear revenue paths.
Maintain diversified crypto exposure; avoid overconcentration in SEC-sensitive tokens; monitor legislative developments.
Long copper and oil futures/ETFs; hedge with put options; monitor LME warehouse data and OPEC+ decisions.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI and quantum computing stocks (NVDA, IBM, GOOGL) on government contracts and secular growth.
opportunities.rationale: U.S. $2B quantum investment and Nvidia's new chips signal sustained AI capex. S&P 500 rally led by AI.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy copper futures or miners (FCX, SCCO) on supply tightness and US premium pull.
opportunities.rationale: Trafigura's LME withdrawals and peace deal uncertainty support copper prices. Green transition demand adds tailwind.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in defense and aerospace (CAE, LMT, RTX) on rising global tensions and drone threats.
opportunities.rationale: CAE profit surge, FBI drone prep for World Cup, SpaceX IPO highlight sector growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in EM currencies (BRL, MXN) and EM equity ETFs (EEM) on peace deal optimism.
opportunities.rationale: Peace hopes boost risk sentiment; dollar stalls. Commodity exporters benefit from copper/oil rally.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Walmart (WMT) on pullback as defensive consumer play with pricing power.
opportunities.rationale: Walmart warns of spending cuts but can pass costs; K-shaped economy favors discount retailers.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore stablecoin-related plays (Visa, Circle) on institutional adoption and settlement tests.
opportunities.rationale: Visa tests stablecoin settlement; institutional demand steadies Bitcoin. Regulatory clarity may improve.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in fixed income; shift to short-term Treasuries and floating-rate notes.
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed rate hike probability rising under Warsh; long bond yields at near two-decade highs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options on S&P 500 (SPY) or VIX calls.
defensive_moves.rationale: Market at record highs with 'jumped the shark' warning from Michael Burry; rate hike risk could trigger correction.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 10-15% to take advantage of potential pullbacks.
defensive_moves.rationale: High bond yields and rate hike uncertainty make cash attractive; credit entry points may improve.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid private credit and high-yield bonds; favor investment-grade corporates and muni bonds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Private credit defaults hit record high; rising rates stress leveraged borrowers.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Short gold (GLD) or buy put options on gold miners as Fed hawkishness persists.
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold drops on Waller's rate hike hint; real yields rising. Safe-haven demand shifting to bonds.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile with a hawkish Fed under Warsh, geopolitical crosscurrents (U.S.-Iran peace deal), and consumer spending slowdown. S&P 500 may see a pullback from overbought levels (8-week winning streak). AI and IPO themes will drive selective rallies. Bond yields could rise further if rate hike expectations solidify. Oil and copper may stay elevated but with high volatility. EM assets could benefit if peace deal materializes.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Positive on AI, quantum computing, and defense sectors driven by structural growth and government support. Consumer staples (Walmart) and discount retailers will outperform discretionary. Copper and energy remain attractive on supply constraints and green transition. However, rising rates and potential recession risk (inverted yield curve, private credit stress) could weigh on broader market. Fed policy normalization under Warsh will be key. SpaceX IPO could be a major catalyst for space/tech. Crypto markets may stabilize with institutional adoption.
key_catalysts: U.S.-Iran peace deal outcome (oil, EM, defense).
Fed rate decision in June/July 2026 (Warsh's first meeting).
SpaceX IPO pricing and demand (likely late 2026).
Nvidia earnings and AI capex guidance.
U.S. consumer spending data (retail sales, confidence).
Copper LME warehouse movements and Chinese demand.
Quantum computing funding and milestones (IBM, others).
Private credit default trends and Fed stress tests.
Global bond yield trajectory (UK, Japan, US).
SEC decisions on crypto ETFs and prediction markets.
watch_list: Fed speeches (Warsh, Waller) for rate hike signals.
SpaceX IPO S-1 amendments and roadshow.
Private credit BDC earnings and default rates.
Muni bond issuance and credit spreads.
